texts.source: Codex Sinaiticus folio + transcription (codexsinaiticus.org) + KJV (bible-api.com)
texts.description: Psalms chapter 9. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 89. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.

texts.source: Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) via handrit.is + Heimskringla.no Old Norse + Thorpe (1866) English

Manuscript folio scans (folios 3r–7v) from handrit.is. Old Norse text verbatim from heimskringla.no. English translation verbatim from Benjamin Thorpe's 1866 edition (Project Gutenberg #14726, public domain).

texts.description: The complete book of Malachi from the Leningrad Codex, the oldest complete Masoretic Hebrew Bible (1008 AD), housed at the Russian National Library, St. Petersburg. Manuscript folios from the public-domain Internet Archive facsimile (pages 644–645). Hebrew text verbatim from the Westminster Leningrad Codex transcription via bolls.life. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611) via bible-api.com.

texts.title: John (Codex Amiatinus)
texts.source: Codex Amiatinus (c. 700 AD) folios via Internet Archive + Latin Vulgate (bolls.life) + Douay-Rheims Bible
texts.description: The complete book of John from Codex Amiatinus, the oldest surviving complete Latin Vulgate Bible, made at Wearmouth-Jarrow in Northumbria around 700 AD and now held at the Biblioteca Medicea Laurenziana in Florence (Amiatino 1). Manuscript folios from the public-domain Internet Archive facsimile (pages 1708–1764). Latin Vulgate text verbatim from bolls.life. English translation verbatim from Douay-Rheims Bible.

texts.title: Tablet I — The Coming of Enkidu
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P273210) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet I — The Coming of Enkidu of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Gilgamesh as tyrant of Uruk; creation of Enkidu by Aruru; Enkidu's wild life and civilisation. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P273210). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure)
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P395404) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet III — The Forest Journey (Departure) of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Preparations for the journey to the Cedar Forest; Ninsun's prayer; the elders' counsel. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P395404). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P397688) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet IV — The Cedar Forest of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Journey to the Cedar Forest; dreams of Gilgamesh; approach to Humbaba's domain. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P397688). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet V — The Combat with Humbaba
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P397688) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet V — The Combat with Humbaba of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Battle with Humbaba, guardian of the Cedar Forest; death of Humbaba. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P397688). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P273210) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet VI — Ishtar and the Bull of Heaven of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Ishtar's proposal to Gilgamesh; his rejection; the Bull of Heaven; death of the Bull. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P273210). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P395404) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet VII — The Death of Enkidu of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Enkidu's dream of the underworld; his illness and death; Gilgamesh's lament. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P395404). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet IX — The Search for Everlasting Life
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P397688) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet IX — The Search for Everlasting Life of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Gilgamesh wanders in grief; journey to the ends of the earth; scorpion-beings at the gate. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P397688). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet X — The Crossing
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P273210) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)
texts.description: Tablet X — The Crossing of the Epic of Gilgamesh. Gilgamesh meets Siduri the tavern-keeper; Urshanabi the ferryman; crossing the waters of death. Tablet photograph from CDLI (Cuneiform Digital Library Initiative, cdli.earth, artifact P273210). Text from Stephen Langdon's edition (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain).

texts.title: Tablet XI — The Flood
texts.source: CDLI tablet photograph (P273210) + Stephen Langdon translation (Project Gutenberg #18897, public domain)

texts.title: Tablet XII — The Return

texts.title: Code of Hammurabi
texts.source: Louvre stele photograph (Wikimedia CC BY 3.0) + L.W. King translation (1910, Yale Avalon Project, PD)
texts.description: The Code of Hammurabi (c. 1754 BCE), the oldest complete law code. 282 laws inscribed on a black diorite stele, now in the Louvre Museum, Paris. Stele photograph from Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 3.0). Translation by L.W. King (1910), from the Yale Avalon Project (public domain).

texts.title: Rigveda, Mandala 1
texts.source: Max Müller editio princeps (1849, archive.org/rigvedasanhitasa03syaauoft) + Ralph Griffith translation (1896, public domain)
texts.description: Mandala 1 of the Rigveda, the oldest religious text still in continuous use (c. 1500-1200 BCE). texts.title: Isaiah (Great Isaiah Scroll, 1QIsa-a)
texts.source: Great Isaiah Scroll (1QIsa-a, c. 125 BCE) photograph from Wikimedia Commons (PD) + Westminster Leningrad Codex Hebrew (bolls.life) + KJV (bible-api.com)
texts.description: Selected chapters of Isaiah from the Great Isaiah Scroll (1QIsa-a), the oldest near-complete biblical manuscript (c. 125 BCE), discovered in Qumran Cave 1 in 1947. The scroll predates all other complete Isaiah manuscripts by over 1,000 years. Now at the Israel Museum's Shrine of the Book, Jerusalem. Photograph from Wikimedia Commons (public domain). Hebrew text from Westminster Leningrad Codex (bolls.life). English from the King James Version (bible-api.com, public domain).

texts.description: The complete book of Genesis from Codex Alexandrinus (5th century AD), one of the earliest and most complete manuscripts of the Greek Bible, now at the British Library. Manuscript page scans from the Frederic Kenyon facsimile (archive.org/codex-alexandrinus, public domain). English from the King James Version (bible-api.com, public domain).

Folio scans via Vatican Library IIIF (digi.vatlib.it). Greek from Tischendorf 8th edition. English from the King James Version (1611, public domain).
